title: Local Jira MCP package infers external mutation + credential env (REVIEW)

origin.description: Single-server config from the public MCP catalog repo khopilot/amazing-mcp-for-productivity (productivity/jira_mcp.json). A local 'npx -y mcp-jira-server' launcher with JIRA_BASE_URL / JIRA_API_TOKEN / JIRA_EMAIL env keys. The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained. All committed env values are already upstream's own placeholders ('your_jira_...'); no real secret was present.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim
no secret redaction needed: env values were already upstream placeholders ('your_jira_instance_url', 'your_jira_api_token', 'your_jira_email')

humanExpectation.rationale: A Jira integration is an external system an autonomous agent could act on (create/transition issues), so the package name 'mcp-jira-server' trips the INFERRED 'action.external-mutation' finding. The credential-shaped JIRA_API_TOKEN env adds 'secrets.env-key', and the unpinned 'npx -y mcp-jira-server' adds 'supply.unpinned-package'. None is a blocker; the honest verdict is REVIEW pending confirmation of which mutating tools are exposed and that the token is sourced safely. This is the second real/redacted case for action.external-mutation (alongside C019 Slack), broadening that thin shape with a different integration domain.
knownLimitations: action.external-mutation is INFERRED from the package name; a read-only Jira reporting integration would not actually mutate anything.

secrets.env-key keys on the credential-shaped name (*_TOKEN), not on whether the committed value is a live secret or a placeholder. JIRA_BASE_URL and JIRA_EMAIL are not credential-shaped and are correctly not flagged.
